what_it_does: Declares a BINDING dict 'by_separator' configuring a radial view where each item's radius is its signed LEAN between two poles (pull toward A vs B), while angle_from still divides the wheel by kind. The two poles are declared variables (pole_a/pole_b) overridable per request, defaulting to two clustering-separated regions of the 'topics' lens. The docstring frames this as a general two-gravity field and names pollution (origin vs AI-corner) as one instance, not its purpose.
observations: BINDING id 'by_separator', label 'Two gravities — what each thing leans toward'.
radius_from='separator' sets radius to a signed lean; angle_from='kind' divides the wheel independently.
Default poles are cluster://topics/k8/6 (worldview/conceptual) and cluster://topics/k8/4 (sessions/runtime).
Comment cites measured separation: 1-cos≈0.19 (widest of 28 centroid pairs), Spearman≈−0.18 opposed ordering.
References a separation_report / discriminator that honestly witnesses whether the poles actually separate.
space='topics' — cosines are computed within ONE space.
Pollution (origin vs AI-corner) named as a deferred application instance using a planted anchor:// vector.
standouts: Generalizes a two-pole resolution into a reusable mechanism, with pollution-detection as just one named instance.
Carries concrete measured separation statistics (1-cos≈0.19, Spearman≈−0.18) and an honest separation_report witness.
conventions: Follows the shared BINDING-dict pattern with id/label/angle_from/radius_from/order_by + meta.
Adds extra keys (pole_a/pole_b plus label fields) specific to this lens.
meta copy is TENTATIVE and operator-facing, consistent with siblings.
notes: The two-gravity separator binding: radius = signed lean between two operator-chosen poles, defaulting to the two most-distinct topics clusters.
questions: How are cluster://topics/k8/N pole references resolved to vectors at render time?
What happens to the default poles if a pyramid rebuild shifts the clusters (does it fail or just re-witness via separates)?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — docstring describes a general variable two-pole radius mechanism and the BINDING encodes exactly that (radius_from='separator', declared overridable poles, single space).
apparent_themes: General mechanism over specific application (pollution as one instance)
Honest self-witnessing (separation_report tells the truth if poles stop separating)
Composability (separator owns only radius; any angular division composes)
intention_signals: Trying to provide a reusable two-pole field that places each thing by which of two operator-chosen gravities it leans toward, designed so the specific (pollution detection) is just a parameterization and the system honestly reports whether the chosen poles really separate.
novelty: Signed-lean radius between two arbitrary, runtime-overridable vector poles, paired with a discriminator that audits whether the poles genuinely separate.
contribution: Contributes the two-gravity/separator binding: the config for a signed two-pole lean radius with overridable poles and a separation witness, composable with any angular division.
summary_for_embedding: A binding-config Python file declaring the 'by_separator' radial view whose radius is each item's signed lean between two overridable vector poles (defaulting to two distinct topics clusters), with a separation-report witness.
intention_for_embedding: To let an operator see what each thing leans toward between two ends they choose, as a general two-gravity field with pollution detection as one instance.
